title: China Is Learning to Use Less Oil—and That's a Bigger Deal Than It Sounds
summary: Three months into the biggest oil supply disruption in modern history, China appears to have discovered something that should make oil bulls at least a little uncomfortable. title: Sanctioned Private Chinese Refiner Seeks Non-Iranian Crude
summary: Hengli Petrochemical, the privately-owned Chinese refiner that was sanctioned by the U.S. in April over allegedly buying Iranian oil, is looking to buy crude from other Middle Eastern producers and West Africa, Reuters reported on Thursday, citing trade sources. title: Why the much-feared crunch in oil markets has yet to arrive
summary: Still-ample stocks and some more reactive producers and consumers look to have left the world better equipped to cope with disruption
